summary: Postinstall exfiltrates environment variables and GitHub Actions OIDC tokens to attacker-controlled ngrok endpoint
details: On npm install, the package runs a postinstall `node -e` one-liner that serializes the full process.env along with os.platform(), os.hostname(), and USER/USERNAME, then POSTs the JSON to the hardcoded ngrok tunnel crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev. When executed inside GitHub Actions, the script additionally reads ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL and ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN, calls the OIDC issuance endpoint with the bearer token to mint an ID token, and adds the resulting oidc_token to the exfil payload sent to the same attacker host. The payload is wrapped with silent error handlers and `|| true` so the install completes without visible errors regardless of network outcome. process.env in CI/dev contexts routinely contains NPM_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, AWS_* and similar secrets; the OIDC token can be exchanged via cloud trust policies for AWS/GCP/Azure credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
